name: Woolly Mammoth
description: Large furry Ice Age mammal that lived on cold grasslands; known for long tusks and thick coat
how_lived: Grazed on grasses in cold open plains with a heavy woolly coat

name: Archaeopteryx
description: Early flying feathered reptile that linked dinosaurs and birds
how_lived: Lived in forests and used feathered wings for short glides and hunting insects

name: Triceratops
description: Herbivorous dinosaur with three horns and a strong protective frill
how_lived: Roamed open plains in herds and used horns to defend against predators

name: Dodo
description: Flightless bird from Mauritius known for being easy to approach and now extinct
how_lived: Foraged on the forest floor for fruit and seeds on a small island

name: Giant Ground Sloth
description: Slow-moving mammal with long claws that lived in South American forests
how_lived: Used long claws to pull down branches and leaves while moving slowly through forests

name: Passenger Pigeon
description: Once extremely common bird in North America that disappeared in the early 1900s
how_lived: Formed huge flocks and fed on seeds and berries in forests

name: Saber-Toothed Cat
description: Large predatory mammal with long canine teeth that hunted prehistoric herbivores
how_lived: Hunted large grazing animals using powerful front legs and long teeth

name: Giant Lemur
description: Large tree-dwelling primate from Madagascar with a slow and gentle lifestyle
how_lived: Moved slowly among forest trees eating leaves and fruit

name: Thylacine
description: Striped marsupial predator also called the Tasmanian tiger, last seen in the 20th century
how_lived: Hunted small animals at night in Tasmania’s forests and grasslands

name: Ichthyosaur
description: Marine reptile shaped like a dolphin that lived in oceans during the age of dinosaurs
how_lived: Lived in the ocean and chased fish with a sleek body and strong tail
